Title: Inactivation of the entire Arabidopsis group II GH3s confers tolerance to salinity and water deficit

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Whole-genome sequencing of gh3oct confirmed eight T-DNA/transposon insertions within GH3.1–GH3.6, GH3.9 and GH3.17 coding regions and only two additional insertions in intergenic regions, which were considered unlikely to contribute to the mutant phenotype; RT-qPCR showed complete loss of full-length GH3 transcripts in gh3oct seedlings with only marginal GH3.9 expression remaining in reproductive tissues, supporting that developmental and stress-related phenotypes arise from loss of group II GH3 function
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of the gh3oct mutant (BGISEQ-500 platform; ~47 million 150-bp reads, 59× depth) to map T-DNA/transposon insertions; reads deposited in SRA under accession SRX8771538
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of GH3.1 together with the other group II GH3s in the gh3oct octuple knockout reduces the plant’s capacity to inactivate IAA (no detectable IAA-Asp, reduced IAA-Glu, oxIAA and oxIAA-glc), leading to high-auxin developmental phenotypes and conferring increased tolerance to salinity and water-deficit stress without major penalties in primary root growth
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Group II GH3-mediated conjugation of IAA to amino acids (especially IAA-Asp and IAA-Glu) provides a major IAA inactivation route; in gh3oct, loss of this pathway abolishes IAA-Asp formation and diminishes IAA-Glu and downstream oxIAA and oxIAA-glc, indicating that a substantial fraction of oxIAA is produced via GH3-dependent conjugates; altered ABA and SA levels under salinity in gh3oct suggest indirect modulation of stress hormone homeostasis
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Auxin (IAA) inactivation pathway via IAA–amino acid conjugation; contributes to hormone-regulated responses to salinity and water-deficit stress, in part through crosstalk with ABA and SA signaling
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II GRETCHEN HAGEN 3 (GH3) family acyl acid amido synthetase
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In seedling roots, GH3 group II transcripts (including GH3.1) are upregulated 6 h after transfer to 75 or 150 mM NaCl; GH3.4 and GH3.9 transcripts are not detected in roots under these conditions
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salinity stress imposed by 50–150 mM NaCl; osmotic stress via sorbitol and mannitol; reduced water potential via PEG8000 perfusion of MS plates; soil water-deficit by withholding irrigation; co-treatment with YUCCA-pathway IAA biosynthesis inhibitors yucasin (20 μM) and PPBo (1 μM) to reduce endogenous IAA; standard MS growth conditions as control
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Auxin (IAA) metabolism and abiotic stress tolerance (salinity and water-deficit/drought) in Arabidopsis development
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity and water-deficit/drought); root system architecture; flowering time and reproductive development
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In 7-d-old seedlings, gh3oct shoots have significantly increased IAA, no detectable IAA-Asp (< LOD), reduced IAA-Glu in shoots and unchanged IAA-Glu in roots, and reduced oxIAA and oxIAA-glc in both organs compared with Col-0 (n=5); gh3oct primary roots maintain similar length to Col-0 but show significantly higher lateral root density throughout 5–12 d; hypocotyl and petiole lengths are significantly increased (P < 0.0001; n ≥ 83 and n = 20, respectively); survival under soil drought after rewatering is ~80% in gh3oct vs ~53% in Col-0; root growth ratios under 50 mM NaCl and PEG-mediated reduced water potential are significantly higher in gh3oct than in Col-0
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: gh3oct plants display epinastic cotyledons, elongated hypocotyls and petioles, increased lateral root density without reduced primary root length, photoperiod-independent early flowering, taller plants with delayed inflorescence arrest, and aberrant flowers and siliques with unfused valves and poor silique elongation
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 wild type; gh3oct octuple knockout (gh3.1,2,3,4,5,6,9,17); individual gh3 single mutants; seedlings grown on MS plates; soil-grown plants for drought assays
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Generation of gh3oct by crossing T-DNA/transposon insertion lines; RT-qPCR to confirm loss of full-length GH3 transcripts; whole-genome resequencing and tagged-sequence mapping of insertions; hormone quantification (IAA, IAA metabolites, JA, JA-Ile, SA, ABA) by UHPLC-MS/MS; root, hypocotyl, petiole and reproductive phenotyping; salinity (NaCl) and osmotic (sorbitol, mannitol, PEG8000) stress assays on plates; soil drought survival assays; co-treatment with YUCCA-pathway IAA biosynthesis inhibitors (yucasin, PPBo); statistical analysis by t-test and ANOVA
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA/transposon insertion knockout (gh3oct octuple mutant including GH3.1); RT-qPCR confirmation of loss of full-length GH3 transcripts; whole-genome resequencing to verify GH3 insertions; phenotypic assays under control and stress conditions; targeted hormone quantification linking loss of GH3 activity to changes in IAA and its metabolites, ABA and SA
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse genetics using existing T-DNA/transposon insertion lines for GH3.1–GH3.6, GH3.9 and GH3.17, combined by crossing to generate the gh3.1,2,3,4,5,6,9,17 (gh3oct) octuple knockout; whole-genome resequencing of gh3oct to map all insertions and confirm disruption of the eight GH3 coding sequences
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Results show that complete inactivation of group II GH3s, including GH3.1, enhances tolerance to salinity and water-deficit stress while maintaining overall viability and a robust, highly branched root system. This supports using GH3.1 and its paralogues as gene-editing targets, for example via stress-inducible or tissue-specific knockdown of group II GH3 activity, to engineer crops with improved salt and drought tolerance and modified root architecture without severely compromising growth.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: In gh3 single mutants, including gh3.2, most IAA metabolites remain close to wild-type levels, underscoring pronounced functional redundancy among group II GH3s; detectable IAA-Glu in gh3oct despite loss of GH3.2 and other group II GH3s suggests the existence of additional GH3 or GH3-like enzymes that catalyze IAA–Glu conjugation
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of gh3oct (includes gh3.2) to map insertion positions; reads deposited as SRA SRX8771538
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elimination of GH3.2 together with the other group II GH3s in gh3oct diminishes IAA inactivation capacity (loss of IAA-Asp, reduced IAA-Glu and oxIAA/oxIAA-glc) and leads to high-auxin phenotypes and enhanced tolerance to salinity and water deficit; in gh3.2 single mutants, IAA concentrations are increased in shoots but decreased in roots, and oxIAA is reduced in shoots, indicating a contribution of GH3.2 to auxin metabolism even when other GH3s are present
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: GH3.2 participates redundantly in the GH3-mediated IAA-amide conjugation pathway, which modulates IAA, IAA-Asp, IAA-Glu and oxIAA levels; combined loss in gh3oct alters auxin and ABA/SA homeostasis associated with stress tolerance
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Auxin inactivation pathway via IAA–amino acid conjugation, contributing to salinity and water-deficit stress responses and hormone homeostasis
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II GRETCHEN HAGEN 3 (GH3) acyl acid amido synthetase
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Group II GH3 genes expressed in roots, including GH3.2, are transcriptionally induced 6 h after transfer to 75 or 150 mM NaCl; GH3.4 and GH3.9 showed no detectable expression in roots under these conditions
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl salinity (0, 50, 75, 150 mM); osmotic stress by osmolytes; PEG8000-induced reduced water potential; soil drought; co-treatment with yucasin and PPBo to modulate endogenous IAA
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Redundant role of group II GH3s in auxin metabolism and abiotic stress (salinity and drought) tolerance in Arabidopsis
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity and water-deficit/drought); root system architecture; flowering time and reproductive development
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In gh3oct (lacking GH3.2), IAA-Asp is below detection and oxIAA and oxIAA-glc are significantly reduced in both shoots and roots, while shoot IAA is increased; gh3oct shows significantly higher root growth ratios under NaCl and PEG and higher drought survival (~80% vs 53% in Col-0); in gh3.2 single mutants, IAA is elevated in shoots and reduced in roots, and oxIAA is reduced in shoots relative to Col-0 (Fig. S7)
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Through its contribution to group II GH3 function, loss of GH3.2 in gh3oct is associated with epinastic cotyledons, elongated hypocotyls and petioles, increased lateral root density, early flowering and abnormal silique development
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 wild type; gh3oct octuple knockout (including gh3.2); gh3.2 single mutant used for metabolite profiling; seedlings grown on MS plates and plants grown on soil
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Construction of gh3oct via crossing gh3.2 and other group II gh3 insertion lines; RT-qPCR and genome resequencing to validate knockouts; hormone quantification (IAA, IAA metabolites, stress hormones) in shoots and roots; phenotyping of root and shoot development and stress responses under NaCl, osmolytes and drought
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of gh3oct octuple knockout (including GH3.2) plus gh3.2 single mutant; RT-qPCR of GH3 transcripts; hormone profiling (IAA and metabolites) in single and octuple mutants; phenotypic assays under salinity and drought stress
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse genetics combining GH3.2 T-DNA/transposon insertion line with other group II gh3 mutants to generate the gh3oct octuple knockout; confirmation of insertions by PCR and whole-genome resequencing
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because GH3.2 contributes redundantly to a GH3 network whose complete inactivation improves salinity and drought tolerance, GH3.2 is part of a promising multi-gene target set for gene-editing strategies aiming to attenuate auxin conjugation specifically under water-stress conditions to enhance stress resilience in crops.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Slight reduction of IAA-Asp in gh3.3 single mutants, contrasted with the complete loss of IAA-Asp in gh3oct, illustrates partial redundancy among group II GH3 enzymes; gh3oct metabolite and stress phenotypes reflect the combined loss of GH3.3 with its paralogues rather than GH3.3 alone
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of gh3oct to confirm the GH3.3 insertion and other group II GH3 lesions (SRA SRX8771538)
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of GH3.3 as part of the gh3oct octuple knockout contributes to the complete absence of IAA-Asp, reduced IAA-Glu and oxIAA/oxIAA-glc, and the resulting high-auxin developmental phenotypes and enhanced tolerance to salinity and water deficit; in gh3.3 single mutants, IAA-Asp is slightly decreased in shoots, indicating a measurable but redundant role in forming IAA-Asp conjugates in vivo
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: GH3.3 participates in the GH3-dependent IAA-amide conjugation route; its combined loss with other group II GH3s shifts auxin catabolism away from amide conjugates and is associated with altered oxIAA, ABA and SA levels and changes in salt and drought tolerance
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Auxin inactivation via IAA–amino acid conjugation, affecting developmental and salinity/drought responses
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II GRETCHEN HAGEN 3 (GH3) acyl acid amido synthetase
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Group II GH3 transcripts in roots, including GH3.3, are induced by NaCl within 6 h, consistent with salt responsiveness of GH3-mediated auxin conjugation
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl stress (up to 150 mM), PEG8000-induced water deficit, soil drought, and osmolyte treatments, under which gh3oct plants (lacking GH3.3) show increased tolerance
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of group II GH3s, including GH3.3, in auxin inactivation and plant tolerance to salinity and water deficit
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity and water-deficit/drought); root system architecture; flowering and reproductive development
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In gh3.3 single mutants, IAA-Asp concentration is slightly decreased in shoots relative to Col-0 (Fig. S7), while overall IAA remains close to wild type; in gh3oct lacking GH3.3, auxin metabolites and stress phenotypes change as described for other group II GH3 members (loss of IAA-Asp, reduced oxIAA/oxIAA-glc, increased stress tolerance)
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Via the gh3oct background, GH3.3 loss is associated with elongated hypocotyls and petioles, increased lateral root density, early flowering and abnormal siliques with unfused valves
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Col-0 wild type; gh3oct octuple knockout lacking GH3.3; gh3.3 single mutant for metabolite profiling; seedlings on MS plates and plants on soil for drought testing
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Generation and confirmation of gh3oct; RT-qPCR of GH3 transcripts; hormone quantification and developmental/stress phenotyping; analysis of IAA metabolites in gh3 single mutants including gh3.3
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of gh3oct knockout including GH3.3 plus gh3.3 single mutant metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR; targeted auxin metabolite quantification; developmental and stress tolerance assays
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Combination of gh3.3 insertion line with other group II gh3 mutants to create the gh3oct octuple knockout; insertions validated by PCR and genome resequencing
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because elimination of the full set of group II GH3s including GH3.3 improves water-stress tolerance, GH3.3 forms part of a GH3 gene module that can be collectively targeted by genome editing or inducible suppression to enhance salinity and drought tolerance in crops.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Lack of detectable GH3.4 expression in roots under NaCl suggests that its contribution to NaCl-induced auxin conjugation is limited in this tissue, even though GH3.4 is genetically disrupted in gh3oct; the phenotypes therefore mostly reflect loss of other GH3s under the tested conditions.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of gh3oct, confirming an insertion within the GH3.4 coding region (data in SRA SRX8771538)
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inactivation of GH3.4 together with the other group II GH3s in gh3oct contributes to the loss of IAA-Asp and reduced IAA-Glu and oxIAA/oxIAA-glc, leading to elevated effective auxin signaling, high-auxin developmental phenotypes and improved tolerance to salinity and water deficit; GH3.4 itself is not detectably expressed in roots under the NaCl conditions tested, suggesting tissue- or condition-specific contribution within the redundant GH3 network
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: GH3.4 forms part of the GH3-mediated IAA-amide conjugation machinery whose absence in gh3oct shifts auxin catabolism away from amide conjugates toward other routes, accompanied by altered ABA and SA levels and enhanced salt and drought tolerance
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Auxin inactivation pathway via GH3-mediated IAA–amino acid conjugation, functioning redundantly with other GH3s in development and stress responses
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II GRETCHEN HAGEN 3 (GH3) acyl acid amido synthetase
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In 10-d-old seedling roots exposed to 0, 75 or 150 mM NaCl for 6 h, GH3.4 transcript was not detected, whereas other group II GH3s were upregulated
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl stress, PEG8000-induced water deficit and soil drought conditions under which gh3oct plants (lacking GH3.4) outperform Col-0; NaCl up to 150 mM is also used to test GH3.4 root expression, where it remains undetectable
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Contribution of GH3.4, as part of group II GH3s, to auxin metabolism and stress-regulated development
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity and water-deficit/drought); root and shoot developmental traits
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: gh3oct (lacking GH3.4) shows the characteristic quantitative changes in IAA metabolites and stress responses (loss of IAA-Asp; reduced oxIAA/oxIAA-glc; increased drought survival from ~53% to ~80%; higher root growth ratios under NaCl and PEG) ascribed to complete group II GH3 loss
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Through participation in the group II GH3 set, GH3.4 loss in gh3oct is associated with epinastic cotyledons, elongated hypocotyls and petioles, denser lateral root systems, early flowering and altered silique development
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Col-0 wild type; gh3oct octuple knockout lacking GH3.4; gh3.4 single mutant (for metabolite profiling); seedlings on MS plates, and soil-grown plants for drought experiments
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RT-qPCR and genome resequencing to confirm GH3.4 disruption in gh3oct; hormonal profiling and phenotyping under control, salinity and water-deficit conditions; RT-qPCR-based expression analysis revealing lack of GH3.4 transcript in roots under NaCl treatment
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of gh3oct knockout including GH3.4; RT-qPCR showing no GH3.4 expression in roots under NaCl; hormone quantification and phenotypic assays to link combined GH3.4 loss to auxin metabolism and stress tolerance
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: GH3.4 insertion line crossed with other group II gh3 mutants to generate gh3oct; insertions confirmed by PCR and whole-genome sequencing
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: As part of the redundant GH3 module, GH3.4 does not need to be individually targeted, but its inclusion in multi-gene knockout or silencing strategies that remove group II GH3 activity can contribute to improved salt and drought tolerance and altered root architecture in crops.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The distinct behavior of gh3.5 single mutants—salt tolerance independent of IAA content and lack of increased PEG tolerance—contrasts with gh3oct, supporting a model in which GH3.5 influences salt tolerance through conjugation of other hormones such as JA and SA, as proposed from prior work cited in the study.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of gh3oct (including GH3.5 lesion) with SRA accession SRX8771538
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In gh3oct, loss of GH3.5 together with other group II GH3s enhances salt and water-deficit tolerance through reduced IAA inactivation and altered hormone profiles; uniquely, gh3.5 single mutants show salt-tolerant root growth, but this tolerance is not suppressed by decreasing endogenous IAA with YUCCA inhibitors, implicating GH3.5 in IAA-independent, possibly SA/JA-related, pathways of salinity tolerance
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: GH3.5 participates in GH3-mediated IAA conjugation but also modulates responses to salinity via IAA-independent mechanisms, as indicated by persistence of salt tolerance in gh3.5 when IAA biosynthesis is inhibited, suggesting regulation through other hormone pathways (e.g. SA or JA) consistent with prior reports cited in the paper
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Auxin inactivation and broader hormone conjugation pathway (GH3.5 affects auxin and, from previous studies cited here, also JA and SA), impacting salt stress responses
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II GRETCHEN HAGEN 3 (GH3) acyl acid amido synthetase
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: GH3 group II genes expressed in roots, including GH3.5, are induced by NaCl in transcript analyses, consistent with salt-responsiveness of GH3.5
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl treatments (for gh3.5 single mutant salt assays and for gh3oct), PEG8000-mediated reduced water potential (where gh3.5 does not differ from Col-0), soil drought, and manipulations of endogenous IAA by yucasin and PPBo to test IAA-dependence of salt tolerance
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Function of GH3.5 in auxin-related metabolism and abiotic stress tolerance, particularly salinity, within the group II GH3 network
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity and water-deficit/drought); root architecture; general growth and development
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under the conditions tested, only gh3.5 single mutants among the gh3 single mutants showed significantly increased salt-tolerant root growth; this enhanced tolerance was not reduced by co-treatment with yucasin or PPBo, unlike the gh3oct phenotype whose NaCl tolerance was abolished by these inhibitors; gh3.5 roots did not show increased tolerance to PEG-mediated water-deficit stress compared with Col-0, in contrast to gh3oct; detailed metabolite changes in gh3.5 are reported in Fig. S7
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Through its inclusion in the group II GH3 set, GH3.5 loss in gh3oct contributes to epinastic cotyledons, elongated hypocotyls and petioles, increased lateral root branching, early flowering and abnormal siliques; gh3.5 single mutants show a salt-tolerant primary root phenotype without clearly altered drought tolerance under PEG or soil drought conditions
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Col-0 wild type; gh3oct octuple knockout lacking GH3.5; gh3.5 single mutant for salt and drought-related assays; plate-grown seedlings and soil-grown plants
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RT-qPCR and whole-genome resequencing to confirm GH3.5 disruption in gh3oct; targeted hormone analyses; salinity tolerance assays on gh3.5 single mutants including co-treatment with IAA biosynthesis inhibitors to test IAA dependence; PEG-based water-deficit assay in gh3.5; comparison with gh3oct phenotypes
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phenotypic comparison of gh3.5 single mutant and gh3oct under NaCl and PEG stress; co-treatment of gh3.5 with IAA biosynthesis inhibitors to test IAA dependence; hormone profiling and developmental phenotyping in the gh3oct background
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Utilization of a GH3.5 insertion mutant both as part of the gh3oct octuple knockout and as a single mutant line to dissect GH3.5-specific contributions to auxin metabolites and salinity tolerance; insertions mapped by PCR and genome resequencing
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: GH3.5 is a particularly attractive target because its loss alone can improve salt tolerance of root growth without the full suite of high-auxin phenotypes seen in gh3oct. Combining GH3.5 manipulation with broader group II GH3 editing could be used to fine-tune crop salt and drought tolerance via both auxin-dependent and auxin-independent hormone pathways.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The reduced IAA-Asp seen in gh3.6 roots but complete loss in gh3oct indicates overlapping specificities among group II GH3s, including GH3.6, for IAA-Asp formation; the major stress-tolerance phenotypes observed are attributed to concurrent loss of all group II GH3s rather than GH3.6 alone.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of gh3oct to confirm GH3.6 insertion (SRA SRX8771538)
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Combined loss of GH3.6 with other group II GH3s in gh3oct abolishes IAA-Asp production, reduces IAA-Glu and oxIAA/oxIAA-glc, and results in high-auxin developmental phenotypes and increased tolerance to salinity and water deficit; gh3.6 single mutants show reduced IAA-Asp in roots, indicating a specific but redundant role in IAA-Asp formation
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: GH3.6 participates in GH3-mediated IAA-conjugation; its loss within gh3oct shifts auxin catabolism away from amide conjugates and is associated with altered ABA and SA accumulation and increased salt and drought tolerance
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Auxin inactivation via GH3-mediated IAA–amino acid conjugation contributing to salinity and drought stress responses
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II GRETCHEN HAGEN 3 (GH3) acyl acid amido synthetase
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: As part of the root-expressed group II GH3 set, GH3.6 transcript is induced by NaCl in seedling roots, based on RT-qPCR analysis of group II GH3s
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl stress regimes, PEG8000-induced water-deficit and soil drought under which gh3oct (lacking GH3.6) outperforms Col-0
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Redundant contribution of GH3.6 to group II GH3-mediated auxin inactivation and plant responses to salinity and water deficit
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity and water-deficit/drought); root system architecture; shoot and reproductive development
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: gh3.6 single mutants have slightly reduced IAA-Asp in roots compared with Col-0 (Fig. S7), whereas gh3oct lacking GH3.6 displays complete loss of IAA-Asp and the characteristic increases in stress tolerance (higher root growth ratios under NaCl and PEG; improved drought survival)
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Through its inclusion in group II GH3s, GH3.6 loss in gh3oct is associated with elongated hypocotyls and petioles, denser lateral root systems, early flowering and abnormal siliques, while primary root length remains similar to wild type
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Col-0 wild type; gh3oct octuple knockout lacking GH3.6; gh3.6 single mutant for IAA metabolite profiling; seedlings on MS plates and soil-grown plants
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Generation and validation of gh3oct; RT-qPCR; hormone quantification in gh3 single mutants including gh3.6; developmental and stress phenotyping of gh3oct
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of gh3oct (including GH3.6 lesion) plus gh3.6 single mutant for IAA metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR; hormone quantification and stress phenotyping to connect GH3.6-containing GH3 network with auxin and stress responses
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: GH3.6 insertion line combined with other group II gh3 mutants to build the gh3oct octuple knockout; insertions confirmed by PCR and genome resequencing
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: GH3.6 is part of the GH3 network whose full inactivation improves salinity and drought tolerance, suggesting that multiplex editing strategies targeting GH3.6 together with other group II GH3s could be used to enhance abiotic stress resilience in crops.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Marginal GH3.9 expression in reproductive tissues of gh3oct indicates incomplete suppression of GH3.9 transcription there, but phenotypic analyses suggest that residual GH3.9 activity is insufficient to maintain normal IAA-Asp or oxIAA levels, reinforcing the key role of the fully intact group II GH3 complement.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of gh3oct confirming GH3.9 disruption (SRA SRX8771538)
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Although GH3.9 is not detectably expressed in roots under NaCl treatment, its disruption along with other group II GH3s in gh3oct contributes to loss of IAA-Asp, reduced IAA-Glu and oxIAA/oxIAA-glc and the resulting high-auxin phenotypes and improved salinity and water-deficit tolerance; marginal GH3.9 expression in reproductive tissues suggests a minor residual role in those organs in the mutant
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: GH3.9 participates in GH3-mediated auxin conjugation but appears to have limited root expression under NaCl in this study; its genetic loss alongside other GH3s in gh3oct contributes to the overall shift in auxin catabolism and hormone balance that enhances stress tolerance
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Auxin inactivation pathway via GH3-mediated IAA conjugation, acting redundantly with other group II GH3s in development and stress responses
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II GRETCHEN HAGEN 3 (GH3) acyl acid amido synthetase
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In NaCl-treated roots, GH3.9 transcript was not detected by RT-qPCR at 0, 75 or 150 mM NaCl, unlike several other group II GH3s; marginal GH3.9 expression remained in reproductive tissues of gh3oct plants
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl treatment for both expression profiling and stress assays; PEG8000-induced water deficit and soil drought conditions for assessing gh3oct performance
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of GH3.9, within group II GH3s, in auxin inactivation and associated stress and developmental responses
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity and water-deficit/drought); plant developmental traits influenced by auxin homeostasis
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No specific quantitative phenotype unique to gh3.9 single mutants is highlighted; in gh3oct lacking GH3.9, the standard metabolite and stress-tolerance shifts (loss of IAA-Asp, reduced oxIAA/oxIAA-glc, higher drought survival and root growth ratios under NaCl/PEG) are observed
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Through collective GH3 loss in gh3oct, GH3.9 disruption is associated with elongated hypocotyls and petioles, increased lateral root density, early flowering and abnormal siliques; the lack of root expression under NaCl suggests GH3.9’s contribution is minor in the root salt response examined here
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Col-0 wild type; gh3oct octuple knockout lacking GH3.9; gh3.9 single mutant for metabolite profiling; MS-grown seedlings and soil-grown plants
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RT-qPCR to assess GH3.9 expression; whole-genome resequencing to verify GH3.9 insertion; hormone quantification and phenotyping in gh3oct; IAA metabolite profiling in gh3.9 single mutant
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR confirming lack of GH3.9 expression in roots under NaCl; detection of marginal GH3.9 expression in reproductive tissues of gh3oct; hormone and phenotypic assays in gh3oct to relate combined group II GH3 loss to auxin metabolism and stress tolerance
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Use of a GH3.9 insertion line combined with other gh3 mutants to generate gh3oct; insertions mapped by PCR and confirmed by whole-genome resequencing
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: GH3.9 is part of the group II GH3 gene set whose collective inactivation enhances salt and drought tolerance, making it one component of a multi-gene editing strategy to reduce auxin conjugation during water stress.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The observation that IAA-Glu remains detectable in gh3oct, despite loss of GH3.17 and all other group II GH3s, provides experimental evidence in Arabidopsis for the existence of additional GH3 or GH3-like IAA–Glu synthetases beyond the classical group II GH3 family.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of gh3oct confirming GH3.17 lesion (SRA SRX8771538)
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of GH3.17 together with the other group II GH3s in gh3oct contributes to the abolition of IAA-Asp, reduction in IAA-Glu and oxIAA/oxIAA-glc, and the resulting high-auxin phenotypes and enhanced tolerance to salinity and water deficit; the persistence of detectable IAA-Glu in gh3oct indicates that auxin–Glu conjugation can still occur without GH3.17 and other group II GH3s, revealing additional GH3 or GH3-like enzymes acting in this pathway
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: GH3.17 participates in GH3-mediated IAA-amide formation, especially IAA–Glu based on prior work; its loss along with other GH3s in gh3oct shifts auxin metabolism and reduces oxIAA and oxIAA-glc levels, supporting a model in which a substantial fraction of oxIAA arises from GH3-dependent conjugates
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Auxin inactivation via IAA–amino acid conjugation, influencing auxin homeostasis and stress responses; GH3.17 is highlighted in the discussion as a key contributor to IAA-Glu formation in prior work, and this study shows that IAA-Glu is still detectable in gh3oct, implying additional GH3-like activities beyond GH3.17
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Group II GRETCHEN HAGEN 3 (GH3) acyl acid amido synthetase
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Group II GH3 transcripts in roots, including GH3.17, are induced by NaCl according to RT-qPCR, supporting salt-responsive regulation of GH3.17
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl salinity, PEG8000-based water-deficit and soil drought conditions under which gh3oct (lacking GH3.17) performs better than Col-0
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of GH3.17 within the group II GH3 ensemble in IAA conjugation and plant tolerance to salinity and drought
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity and water-deficit/drought); auxin-regulated root and shoot development
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In gh3oct, despite complete loss of detectable IAA-Asp, IAA-Glu is decreased but still detectable in shoots and unchanged in roots relative to Col-0, implying that GH3.17 and other group II GH3s are not the sole contributors to IAA-Glu formation; gh3oct also shows the characteristic increases in stress tolerance detailed for the group II GH3 set (root growth ratios and drought survival)
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Through its inclusion in the group II GH3 gene set, GH3.17 loss in gh3oct is associated with high-auxin phenotypes such as elongated hypocotyls and petioles, increased lateral root branching, early flowering and abnormal silique development
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Col-0 wild type; gh3oct octuple knockout lacking GH3.17; gh3.17 single mutant for IAA metabolite profiling; seedlings on MS plates and plants on soil
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RT-qPCR and genome resequencing to confirm GH3.17 disruption in gh3oct; auxin metabolite profiling in gh3 single mutants including gh3.17; developmental and stress phenotyping and hormone quantification in gh3oct
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of gh3oct octuple knockout (including GH3.17 lesion); hormone profiling demonstrating altered IAA, IAA-Glu, oxIAA and oxIAA-glc; developmental and stress phenotyping to connect combined GH3.17 loss to auxin metabolism and stress tolerance
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: GH3.17 insertion line combined with other group II gh3 mutants to generate gh3oct; insertions confirmed by PCR and whole-genome resequencing
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because GH3.17 is part of a GH3 module whose complete removal enhances tolerance to salinity and water deficit, but is not solely responsible for IAA–Glu formation, it should be considered within multiplex editing strategies targeting several GH3 genes to modulate auxin conjugation and stress tolerance in crops.
